Four targeted blocks covering the most intensive animations. Character wander/bob keyframes in Office.css not yet covered.

ideas.title: Streak Eulogy Border Is Still Purple — type: info Has Never Been Read by the Renderer
ideas.description: App.jsx:979 — `setCompletionToast({ text: '💀 RIP the streak…', type: 'info' })`. App.jsx:1129 — the render reads `completionToast.color || '#6366f1'`. The `.type` field is never consulted. The `.color` field is never set on the eulogy path. Result: the death announcement glows in the same indigo as every shipping celebration. Review #73 claimed this was fixed in its `fixed_since_last_review` entry. The claim is false — no type-check branch exists in the current render code. Fix: in the completionToast style block, branch on `completionToast.type === 'info'` for border and boxShadow: use `var(--text-muted, #6b7280)` and `rgba(107,114,128,0.12)` respectively. One ternary per property, one line each. WHY: the data for grief styling has been set for four review cycles. The renderer just needs to look at it.

ideas.title: wanderTooltips Still Tells the Wrong Story to the Wrong Person
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1480-1486 — `wanderTooltips = { 1: '☕ Heading to the coffee corner', 2: '💬 Lingering by the whiteboard', 3: '🪟 Staring out the window', 4: '🧘 Taking a mindful moment' }` keyed by `(i % 4) + 1` where `i` is the position in idleWorkers that poll cycle. If Carlos is second idle, he gets the whiteboard. If Luna is third, she gets the window. The tooltip is the only hover moment in the entire office view where a visitor can read character identity — and it's a slot machine. Fix: add `WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS` keyed by worker.id: `{ carlos: 'Probably chasing down that weird log entry', maya: 'Sketching something in her head', dave: 'Headphones on — he can hear you', sofia: 'Found something. She always finds something.', luna: 'Horizon-scanning. Leave her to it.' }`. In the idle render at line 1493, use `WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS[w.id] ?? wanderTooltips[wIdx]`. WHY: flagged in reviews #72 and #73, two consecutive must-priority entries, still zero action.

ideas.title: Idle Micro-Bubble Emoji Picker Synchronizes All Workers to the Same Array Index
ideas.description: Office.jsx:739 — the idle bubble renders: `(WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES[worker.id] || DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES)[Math.floor(Date.now() / 12000) % length]`. The index is derived from Unix time divided by 12 seconds — a global clock shared by every Character instance. At any given 12-second window, all idle workers show the same positional index from their respective arrays. Carlos and Maya don't independently daydream; they are puppets on the same timer. At 12:00:00 both show index 0 (Carlos: ☕, Maya: 💜). At 12:00:12 both show index 1 (Carlos: 📊, Maya: ✏️). The arrays are character-specific (correctly fixed in review #71) but the picker throws that specificity away. Fix: replace the time-based index with a worker-local counter. In the idle bubble useEffect, instead of `setIdleBubble(true)`, call `setIdleBubble(pick())` where `pick()` increments a `useRef` counter modulo the array length. The character picks their next thought independently when their personal timer fires. WHY: WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES was added to give each character a voice. A synchronized clock makes it invisible.

ideas.title: ActivityLog Internal Slice(0,20) Is Invisible to the Header Count
ideas.description: App.jsx ActivityLog component line 135: `const displayed = [...entries].reverse().slice(0, 20)`. The component always caps at 20 regardless of how many filtered entries are passed. The section header at App.jsx:1319 computes `filtered.length` from the full filter expression and shows e.g. '25 of 50 events (filtered)'. If there are 25 system events but ActivityLog silently shows only 20, the user sees the header say '25' while the list contains 20 — a discrepancy with no explanation. Fix option A: pass a `maxEntries` prop to ActivityLog (default 20) and reference it in the slice; update the header count to `Math.min(filtered.length, maxEntries)` with a note '(first 20 shown)'. Fix option B: add a load-more button inside ActivityLog that expands the slice in increments of 10. WHY: the header is the trust anchor for the log. When it reports a different number than the user sees, they don't know whether they're missing information. And on a team with many vibe transitions in a single day, they are.

ideas.title: ProgressBar Dead updatedAt Prop — Signature Promises Data It Never Uses
ideas.description: App.jsx:194 — `function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt })`. `updatedAt` is destructured and passed at WorkerCard line 347 as `updatedAt={worker.updated_at}`. Inside ProgressBar (lines 195-240), the identifier `updatedAt` does not appear once. `startedAt` is used for ETA calculation. `updatedAt` was presumably intended for a staleness indicator but was never wired. Flagged in reviews #71 and #72 as should-priority, unfixed. Two paths: (A) delete `updatedAt` from the destructure and the call site — clean the contract; (B) fulfill the original intent and render a subtle 'stalled' indicator when `updatedAt` is set, `progress < 100`, and `Date.now() - new Date(updatedAt).getTime() > 1_800_000` (30 minutes of no progress change). Path B converts a dead prop into a live warning. WHY: a function parameter that accepts data and ignores it tells the next developer that data mattered here. It didn't. The contract should be honest.

ideas.title: AwaySign Sticky Note Speaks in the Voice of Nobody — Still Generic After Four Reviews
ideas.description: Office.jsx:435-439 — `AwaySign` picks from `AWAY_MESSAGES = ['☕ Coffee run', '🧠 Deep think', '📞 On a call', …]` using a deterministic seed from `workerId + idleMinutes`. The same desk at the same idle duration always shows the same generic message regardless of who's sitting there. Six lines below, at Office.jsx:1394, the nameplate renders `WORKER_AWAY_DESK_LABELS[occ.worker.id]` — character-specific copy: carlos gets '↪ in the logs', luna gets '↪ reviewing'. Two systems, two personalities, one desk, right next to each other. The sticky note is larger, animated, and more visible than the nameplate. It should be the primary voice. Fix: add `WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES` keyed by worker.id — `{ carlos: ['☕ In the logs', '🔌 Debugging', '📖 Reading docs'], maya: ['🎨 Sketching', '✏️ Wireframing', '💜 Mood-boarding'], dave: ['🎧 Headphones in', '⚙️ Pipeline check', '📦 Infra thinking'], sofia: ['🔍 Exploring', '🧪 Test design', '📋 Case writing'], luna: ['🌙 Daydreaming', '✨ Spec drafting', '💡 Ideas percolating'] }`. In `AwaySign`, pick from `WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES[workerId]?.[seed % 3] ?? AWAY_MESSAGES[idx]`. WHY: the sticky note is the soul of the idle desk. It should know who left it.

ideas.title: Office Window at 10PM Is a Purple Void — Stars Only Render at Midnight
ideas.description: Office.jsx:791 — `const isMidnight = hour >= 0 && hour <= 4`. Star spans render only inside the `{isMidnight && <> … </>}` block (lines 800-807). The evening block (hour >= 20 && hour <= 23) renders a deep purple gradient `('#4c1d95' to '#312e81')` with nothing inside it. A 10pm sky viewed through a real window has stars. The midnight path already has the pattern: four `<span className='office-window__star'>` elements with staggered positions. Extend: add a `const isEvening = hour >= 20 && hour <= 23` and render 2-3 stars at slightly sparser positions (e.g., left: 25%, 62%, 44% — avoiding the 4-star density of midnight). WHY: the window is a time clock. At 10pm it should look like night outside. Right now the evening sky is a solid purple rectangle that looks like an error state, not a view.

ideas.title: Whiteboard Says 'avg task progress' When One Person Is the Entire Average — Flagged 14 Reviews Ago
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1278 — `avg task progress` renders unconditionally whenever `workingWorkers.length > 0`. When exactly one worker is active, their progress IS the average by definition, but the label calls it an average anyway. Flagged in review #60 as a 'should' item ('The Whiteboard Calls It an Average With One Person in the Room'). 14 reviews have passed. The fix is one ternary: `{workingWorkers.length === 1 ? 'task progress' : 'avg task progress'}`. One JSX operator. WHY: this is the oldest unfixed idea in the file and it is a one-word change. If 14 review cycles can't move a one-word fix, something is wrong with the triage process.

ideas.title: Ficus prevPlantStageRef Is Structurally Stuck at Seedling — Grief Detection Is Architecturally Impossible
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1101-1110 — the ficus milestone useEffect: `if (prevPlantStageRef.current !== plantStage && plantStage !== 'seedling') { setPlantMilestone(true); …; return () => … }`. The early `return` exits the effect body, which means `prevPlantStageRef.current = plantStage` (line 1109) is never reached during a growth event. The ref stays at 'seedling' after initial mount. When the streak drops and `plantStage` reverts to 'seedling', the check `prevPlantStageRef.current !== plantStage` evaluates `'seedling' !== 'seedling'` = false — and the `plantStage !== 'seedling'` guard then has nothing to negate. No grief fires. This has been flagged since review #65 — nine reviews. The architectural reason is finally identifiable: the ref needs to be updated INSIDE the if branch (add `prevPlantStageRef.current = plantStage` before the return), and a separate grief clause needs its own check: `if (prev !== 'seedling' && plantStage === 'seedling') { /* grief toast */ }`. Two insertions fix a nine-review-old wound. WHY: the plant levels up with fanfare; it should regress with acknowledgment. The asymmetry makes the streak feel like a decoration rather than a living thing.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Counter Opens at 1/9 on Fresh Load — Visitor Told They Discovered Something They Haven't
ideas.description: App.jsx:1390 — the counter formula `(quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1` with `BONUS_TAGLINES.length = 8` produces denominator 9. On fresh load, `quoteBonus = 0`, so the counter reads `(0 % 9) + 1 = 1`, displayed as '1/9'. The visitor is on the default vibe tagline with zero clicks logged, yet the UI tells them they have seen 1 of 9 quotes. The denominator 9 bundles the default vibe tagline into the collection count, which is not a bonus discovery. Fix: show no counter when `quoteBonus === 0`. Once the user has clicked once, show `quoteBonus / BONUS_TAGLINES.length` — the true bonus-only count (1/8, 2/8, etc.). Alternatively, display `✦` (the sparkle) alone until first click, then switch to numbered format. WHY: discovery counters only delight when accurate. A wrong counter teaches visitors that UI numbers are decorative.

ideas.title: vibeCaption Has No Cooldown — Oscillating Teams Spam the Overlay Every 30 Seconds
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1180-1192 — `setVibeCaption` fires on every `[vibe]` change with a 2800ms auto-clear and no minimum interval guard. If one worker toggles between working and idle every poll cycle (30s), the vibe oscillates between 'in-flow' and 'slow-day', and the caption fires every 30 seconds indefinitely: '⚡ In the zone' → '☁️ Easy does it' → '⚡ In the zone'. The `isFirstVibe` guard correctly skips the initial mount but provides no per-transition cooldown. Fix: add `const lastCaptionTsRef = useRef(0)` and gate the setVibeCaption call: `if (Date.now() - lastCaptionTsRef.current < 90_000) return; lastCaptionTsRef.current = Date.now()`. 90 seconds between captions. WHY: the vibe caption is a punctuation mark, not a ticker. When it fires every 30 seconds it becomes ambient noise. When it fires every 90+ seconds, it's a genuine announcement.

ideas.title: TASK_TAG_DEFS Has No Planning or Meeting Tag — A Whole Category of Work Stays Invisible
ideas.description: App.jsx:164-172 — TASK_TAG_DEFS covers Bug, Ship, Design, QA, Data, and Docs. Tasks like 'roadmap planning', 'sprint standup prep', 'sync with stakeholders', 'retro facilitation', 'quarterly kickoff' match nothing and render no tag. This is particularly relevant for Bloberto (Manager) and Luna (Creative Director) whose work skews heavily toward planning and alignment. Add: `{ pattern: /plan|roadmap|sprint|standup|retro|kickoff|sync|agenda|meeting|align|discuss/i, label: 'Plan', emoji: '🗓️', className: 'task-tag--plan' }`. In App.css add `.task-tag--plan { background: rgba(234,179,8,0.15); color: #ca8a04; }`. WHY: the tag system is the dashboard's semantic layer. A category that affects a third of the team's time and appears in zero tags makes the system feel like it was designed for engineers only.

ideas.title: The Conference Table Should Know How Long the Meeting Has Been Going
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1152-1158 — when `meetingWorkers.length >= 2`, `lastHuddle.current.endedAt` stays null and the 'Huddle' badge renders with no duration. A meeting that has been going 47 minutes looks identical to one that just started. There's already a `lastHuddle` ref tracking names and endedAt — adding a start time is trivial. Add `startedAt: null` to the lastHuddle ref initial value, set it alongside the names capture when `meetingWorkers.length >= 2` transitions from <2 to >=2. In the `conf-meeting-badge` render, show duration: a `useMemo` or inline computation from `Date.now() - lastHuddle.current.startedAt` formatted as 'Xm'. Render as '🤝 Huddle · 12m'. At 30 minutes, escalate: 'Huddle · 30m ☕' (someone should check on them). At 60 minutes, the badge turns red: 'Huddle · 1h 😬'. WHY: meeting duration is the universal office anxiety. The conference table should participate in that feeling. It is already the most social piece of furniture in the office.

theme: The ghost of review #74 still haunts six open tickets — and the clock on the wall is lying to everyone
visual_observations: Puppeteer timed out; full code review of App.jsx, Office.jsx performed instead. Findings: (1) WallClock uses setInterval(60000) anchored to page-load time, not to the real clock boundary — the clock can display the wrong minute for up to 59s after it turns. (2) WindowElement.getGradient returns a full-night purple gradient for hours 20-23 but timeLabel calls those same hours 'dusk' — the aria-label and the sky color disagree. (3) Stars only render for midnight (hours 0-4); the 8pm–11pm purple sky is empty. (4) sparkleUnseen on line 748 of App.jsx fires localStorage.setItem on mount before any user interaction, permanently killing the footer twinkle effect. (5) idleBubble emoji picker (line 747) still divides Date.now() by 12000 globally, meaning all idle workers flash the same emoji index in unison every 12 seconds. (6) vibeCaption in Office.jsx line 1197 fires on every vibe change with no cooldown guard — oscillating teams still spam the overlay. (7) Footer quote counter starts at 1/9 on fresh load. (8) TASK_TAG_DEFS still has no 'Plan' or 'Meeting' pattern — fourth review in a row. (9) completionToast border fix appears to be LIVE in current code (line 1130 checks type === info correctly) — reviews 72 and 74 may have reviewed stale code. (10) VIBE_VERSIONS IS wired to footer at line 1392 — another previously-flagged bug that is actually fixed.

ideas.title: sparkleUnseen Marks Itself Dead on Mount — The Twinkle Never Fires
ideas.description: App.jsx line 748: `useEffect(() => { if (sparkleUnseen) localStorage.setItem('footer-sparkle-seen', '1') }, [sparkleUnseen])` fires on the very first render because sparkleUnseen is initialised as `!localStorage.getItem(...)` and the effect has no guard. The moment any visitor loads the page, the twinkle is killed before they can see it. Fix: only write to localStorage when the user actually interacts with the footer sparkle (onMouseEnter or onClick on the footer-tagline span), not on mount.

ideas.title: WallClock Drifts Up to 59 Seconds from Real Time
ideas.description: Office.jsx WallClock component (line 1003): `setInterval(() => setTime(new Date()), 60000)` is anchored to the page-load instant. If the page loads at 3:47:45, the interval fires at 4:47:45, 5:47:45, etc. — meaning from 3:48 to 4:47 the clock shows 3:47. Fix: compute milliseconds until the next whole minute on mount and use a `setTimeout` to align to the clock boundary, then start the 60s interval from there. This is a 4-line fix that makes the clock actually trustworthy.

ideas.title: Window Sky Says 'Dusk' But Renders Full Night — timeLabel and Gradient Disagree for Hours 20-23
ideas.description: Office.jsx WindowElement (lines 791-802): getGradient returns `linear-gradient(to bottom, #4c1d95, #312e81)` (full night purple) for hours 20-23, but timeLabel evaluates to 'dusk' for those same hours (the condition chain exhausts at `hour <= 19 ? 'golden hour' : 'dusk'`). The aria-label says 'dusk sky' while the user sees a midnight-purple sky. Either extend timeLabel to return 'evening' or 'night' for 20-23, or shift the gradient boundary. This also means stars should render at 20-23 if we call it night — a bug flagged in reviews 72 and 74 with the same root: the star condition (line 799) uses `isMidnight = hour >= 0 && hour <= 4` and never includes evening.

ideas.title: idleBubble Emoji Index Is Still a Global Clock — Third Review
ideas.description: App.jsx line 747: `Math.floor(Date.now() / 12000) % length` uses wall-clock time as the index, so all idle workers flip to the next emoji at the exact same millisecond. The fix (suggested in review 74) is to seed the offset with `wanderIdx` or the worker's character code sum so each character independently cycles their emoji array. One line: `Math.floor((Date.now() + (worker.id?.charCodeAt(0) ?? 0) * 4000) / 12000) % length`.

ideas.title: vibeCaption Has No Cooldown — Oscillating Vibe Still Spams the Office Floor
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1188-1200: vibeCaption fires on every vibe change with isFirstVibe guarding only the mount. A team hovering between 'in-flow' and 'slow-day' every 30 seconds will show the caption overlay continuously. Add a `lastVibeCaptionRef = useRef(0)` and only fire if `Date.now() - lastVibeCaptionRef.current > 90_000`. This is the third consecutive review flagging this.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Counter Opens at 1/9 on Fresh Load — Visitor Told They Discovered Something They Haven't
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1391: `(quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1` evaluates to 1 when quoteBonus is 0. The visitor hasn't clicked anything yet, but the counter says '1/9'. This implies there are 9 things to discover and they found the first one automatically. Fix: show the counter only after the first click — `quoteBonus > 0 && ...{quoteBonus}/{BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1}`. Fourth consecutive review flagging this.

ideas.title: TASK_TAG_DEFS Missing Plan/Meeting Pattern — Five Reviews, Zero Progress
ideas.description: App.jsx line 165-173: TASK_TAG_DEFS has Bug, Ship, Design, QA, Data, Docs patterns. A 'Plan' or 'Meeting' tag was added for keywords like standup/kickoff/meeting (line 172), but reviewing the actual code it IS there: `plan|roadmap|sprint|kickoff|standup|meeting|agenda|discuss|ceremony|sync`. So this specific idea is already fixed. The remaining gap: there is no 'Refactor' or 'Performance' tag. Worker tasks containing 'refactor|perf|optimize|benchmark|profil' produce no tags, making an entire category of engineering work invisible on the dashboard.

ideas.title: Greeting Voice and Vibe Are Divorced — 'Good Morning' During an Incident Is Jarring
ideas.description: App.jsx lines 1086-1093: the greeting ('Good morning', 'Good afternoon', etc.) is time-only and completely ignores teamVibe. When the vibe is 'on-fire', 'Good morning, captain 🚨' is the right energy. When vibe is 'after-hours', 'Burning the midnight oil' already exists. Add a vibe override layer: if vibe === 'on-fire', greet with '🚨 Brace yourself' regardless of hour; if vibe === 'crushing', greet with '🚀 Let's go'. This collapses a logical inconsistency and makes the header feel alive and reactive.

ideas.title: Conference Table Should Show Elapsed Meeting Time When Huddle Is Active
ideas.description: Office.jsx ConferenceTable (line 833): the component receives meetingWorkers and knows exactly when the huddle started (via lastHuddle.current) but never displays duration while the meeting is live. Only the 'last huddle' label shows post-meeting. Add a local useState for elapsed minutes, tick every 60s, and display '⏱ 12m' inside the conference-table SVG when meetingWorkers.length >= 2. People watching the dashboard will finally know if the standup has been going for 5 minutes or 45. This is a wild idea from review 74 that's actually a 'should' — one-person team will love it.

ideas.title: Add a 'Refactor' TASK_TAG — The Invisible Engineering Category
ideas.description: App.jsx TASK_TAG_DEFS: add `{ pattern: /refactor|perf|optimize|benchmark|profil|cleanup/i, label: 'Refactor', emoji: '🔵', className: 'task-tag--refactor' }` and a matching CSS class. Currently a task like 'Refactor auth module for performance' produces zero tags, making refactor work invisible in the task-tag filter system. This is the engineering iceberg — the work nobody sees.

ideas.title: Bloberto's Title Changes Per Vibe but the Crown Never Does — Add a Crisis Crown
ideas.description: Office.jsx BLOBERTO_TITLES (line 100) gives Bloberto context-sensitive job titles (CVO, CFO, etc.), which is great. But the crown drawn at CharacterAvatar line 243 is always the same gold polygon. When vibe is 'on-fire', the crown should be crimson-tipped or animated with a tiny flame; when 'slow-day', it droops slightly (transform: rotate(10deg) on one point). The crown is the most visible part of Bloberto — it should react to the chaos it's presiding over.

ideas.title: Ghost Worker Click Should Jump to Their Last Activity Entry
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1379: ghost desks show OOO bubble with 'Last seen: Xh ago' on hover. But clicking a ghost character does nothing — onWorkerClick is not wired for ghost = true (line 1475: `onClick={!occ.ghost && onWorkerClick ? handleCharClick : undefined}`). Clicking a ghost should switch to the Dashboard tab and scroll to / highlight their most recent activity entry. This turns the OOO bubble from a tombstone into a portal — 'where did they go?' becomes answerable.

ideas.title: Stuck Worker Card Gets a Heartbeat — The Dashboard Already Has the Data
ideas.description: App.jsx StatsBar (line 443-444): stuckWorkers are detected (working > 2h) and shown in the stats bar. But on the worker cards themselves, 'stuck' workers only get a data-tier='stuck' attribute on their duration badge and the '⚠️' prefix. There's no visual pulse or animation drawing the eye. Add a CSS keyframe `stuck-pulse` that gently breathes the worker-card border between red-dim and red-bright at ~2s cycle. The stats bar already warns you — the card should too.

ideas.title: The Office Wall Should Have a Motivational Poster That Changes With the Vibe
ideas.description: The office floor has a whiteboard, a clock, a window, a door, a coffee corner, and desks — but the walls are empty. Add a tiny motivational poster SVG in the top-right wall area. Each vibe gets its own poster text: 'crushing' → 'HUSTLE HARDER (just kidding, pace yourself)'; 'on-fire' → 'HANG IN THERE 🔥'; 'slow-day' → 'IS IT FRIDAY YET?'; 'in-flow' → 'DEEP WORK IN PROGRESS'; 'after-hours' → 'GO HOME.'. It's 20 lines of SVG and it makes the office feel inhabited rather than simulated.

ideas.title: Press 'B' to Bloberto — A Hidden Boss Key That Spotlights the Manager
ideas.description: App.jsx SHORTCUT_KEYS (line 593) currently handles 1, 2, R, Escape. Adding 'b' to focus the Bloberto character with a short flash + speech bubble would be delightful. When pressed, it triggers a ping on Bloberto (the only character you can't currently click in the office). Bloberto responds with a vibe-specific speech bubble: 'crushing' → 'Numbers are beautiful today.' 'on-fire' → 'I've seen worse. (I haven't.)' etc. It's a hidden easter egg for power users who already know shortcuts exist.

hire_proposals.reason: Nine bugs have now persisted across three or more consecutive reviews without being fixed. The team is shipping features faster than bug fixes. A dedicated reliability role — someone who owns the fix queue — would break this pattern. They wouldn't build new features; they would own the debt list and close it.

screenshot_note: Puppeteer succeeded — screenshot at /workspace/screenshot-luna-76.png. Captured at approximately 00:31 UTC on Tuesday Mar 10. Vibe: Crushing It. Time: after-hours.
theme: Five bugs survived review 75's mass-fix pass — and the WallClock pattern we just applied has a twin we missed
visual_observations: Header shows '🌃 Burning the midnight oil, night owl 🌙 · Tuesday, Mar 10' with vibe pill '🔥 Crushing It'. The honorific 'night owl' is from HONORIFICS['after-hours'] because isAfterHours=true (hour 0) overrides the vibe lookup entirely. The team is crushing it at midnight and the greeting calls them a night owl instead of 'LEGEND 🔥'. Confirmed visually. Flagged review 75, still not fixed.

Bloberto's title reads 'CVO' — correct for crushing vibe. Speech bubble shows 'Wait you shipped at THIS hour?! Legends. 🏆' — the after-hours + crushing vibe combination produces a context-aware reaction. This is one of the most delightful interactions in the whole app.

Office window (top right) shows 'MIDNIGHT' label with four star dots — correct for hours 0–4. This confirms star rendering works at midnight. The same window at 10pm (hours 20–23) renders an identical purple gradient but shows 'dusk' and zero stars. Still unfixed since review 72.

Coffee corner reads 'COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT' — this comes from Office.css line 1668: `[data-vibe='crushing'] .coffee-corner__label::after { content: ' TRIPLE SHOT'; }`. Also: 'on-fire' → ' STRONG. NOW.', 'slow-day' → ' ...zzz', 'after-hours' → ' (decaf? jk)'. This is a CSS micro-detail I have never called out in any review. It is extremely good.
The keyboard shortcut toast is visible: '⌨️ Shortcuts: 1 Office · 2 Dashboard · R Refresh · Esc Clear' — appearing correctly on first load in a fresh browser context.
Luna's nameplate is highlighted in bright purple — she appears recently pinged or is the active focused worker. The ping-and-jump-to-dashboard flow is working as expected.
The ficus and decorative plants are visible in the office floor. No growth toast is visible in the screenshot — vibeStreak would need to be 2+ in a fresh session to trigger this.

Bloberto's vibe-weather reads '☀️ Clear skies — team at full strength' in the bottom-right of the office floor. This is correct for crushing vibe but feels tonally disconnected at midnight — 'clear skies' is a daytime metaphor. The weather system has no time-of-day layer.

fixed_since_last_review.title: sparkleUnseen No Longer Kills Itself on Mount
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx line 711: `const [sparkleUnseen] = useState(() => !localStorage.getItem(...))` — the useEffect that wrote to localStorage on mount is gone. Writing only happens inside the onClick handler (line 1387). The twinkle now survives until the user actually clicks.

fixed_since_last_review.title: WallClock Now Aligns to Minute Boundary
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx: `const msUntilNextMinute = 60000 - (Date.now() % 60000)` — uses setTimeout to land on the real minute boundary, then starts the 60s interval. Cleanup correctly calls clearTimeout AND clearInterval. Textbook fix.

fixed_since_last_review.title: wanderTooltips Now Keyed by worker.id
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS map inside idleWorkers.map — each character has their own tooltip array. Carlos gets '📊 Reviewing the logs', Maya gets '💜 Studying the color palette', etc. Four reviews closed at once.

fixed_since_last_review.title: AwaySign Now Uses WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx line 436: WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES keyed by worker ID — Carlos gets sticky notes about logs and debugging; Luna gets 'spec drafting' and 'ideas percolating'. The soul of the idle desk is now character-specific.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Luna SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES Covers All 5 Vibes
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx line 268: luna now has on-fire ('🔥 One creative in a burning building'), in-flow ('✨ Ideas arrive faster when the office goes quiet'), slow-day ('🌙 Slow day. The perfect kind for the ideas that need silence.'), plus crushing and after-hours.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Activity Log System Filter Is Live
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx: `{ key: 'system', label: '⚙️ System' }` is in the filter array. Vibe transition events (type:'system') are now filterable. Four years of 'should' finally landed.

ideas.title: WindowElement Interval Not Clock-Aligned — WallClock's Twin Bug
ideas.description: Office.jsx WindowElement line 783: `setInterval(() => { const h = getHour(); setHour(prev => prev === h ? prev : h) }, 60000)` — same pattern WallClock had and just got fixed. If the page loads at 19:59:45, the sky shows 'golden hour' gradient until 20:00:45, then snaps to 'dusk' 45 seconds late. Fix: apply the exact same msUntilNextMinute pattern just merged for WallClock: `const ms = 60000 - (Date.now() % 60000); setTimeout(() => { setHour(getHour()); id = setInterval(..., 60000) }, ms)`. Two-minute fix. The pattern is already in the codebase.

ideas.title: Greeting Honorific Overridden by Time-of-Day — Confirmed Visually
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1155: `HONORIFICS[isAfterHours ? 'after-hours' : (teamVibe.key === 'after-hours' ? 'slow-day' : teamVibe.key)]` — isAfterHours=true at 00:31 forces 'after-hours' key, returning 'night owl 🌙'. The team's vibe is 'Crushing It' (LEGEND 🔥). The screenshot confirms: 'Burning the midnight oil, night owl 🌙' next to the '🔥 Crushing It' pill. Fix: use `HONORIFICS[teamVibe.key] ?? 'boss'` directly — the greeting text already carries the time-of-day context, the honorific should reflect the energy.

ideas.title: Window Sky Hours 20–23: 'Dusk' Label, Night Gradient, Zero Stars — Fourth Review
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 802: `timeLabel = ... hour <= 19 ? 'golden hour' : 'dusk'`. For hours 20–23 the gradient is `linear-gradient(to bottom, #4c1d95, #312e81)` (deep purple night) but the aria-label says 'dusk sky' and `isMidnight` (line 799) only covers 0–4, so no stars render. Fix in three lines: (1) add `const isEvening = hour >= 20 && hour <= 23` alongside isMidnight, (2) update timeLabel to return 'evening' or 'night' for those hours, (3) render stars for both isMidnight and isEvening. The screenshot tonight shows the midnight window looks beautiful with four stars — that same window at 10pm is an empty purple void.

ideas.title: idleBubble Emoji Picker Still a Global Clock — Fourth Consecutive Review
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 747: `Math.floor(Date.now() / 12000) % length` — all idle workers cycle their emoji arrays simultaneously. Carlos shows ☕ at the exact second Maya shows 💜 and Sofia shows 🔍 — because all three hit the same global index. The one-line fix (suggested three reviews ago): `Math.floor((Date.now() + (worker.id?.charCodeAt(0) ?? 0) * 4000) / 12000) % length`. This drifts each character's cycle by their character-code offset, making idle moments feel genuinely independent. This is the fourth review flagging this. Escalating to 'should'.

ideas.title: Ficus Grief Toast Structurally Impossible — Eleven Reviews
ideas.description: Office.jsx ficus milestone useEffect: the early return when `prevPlantStageRef.current !== plantStage && plantStage !== 'seedling'` fires the toast but never reaches `prevPlantStageRef.current = plantStage`. The ref stays at 'seedling' forever. When vibeStreak drops and plantStage returns to 'seedling', `'seedling' !== 'seedling'` is false — no grief toast fires. Fix: add `prevPlantStageRef.current = plantStage` before the early return (inside the if-branch). The grief toast has been broken since review 65. This is eleven reviews. That is not a 'could'. That is a ghost.

ideas.title: vibeCaption Still Has No Cooldown — Fourth Consecutive Review
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1188–1200: the vibeCaption useEffect fires on every vibe change with no throttle guard. An oscillating team (30s poll, two vibes flip-flopping) will show the overlay every 30 seconds. Fix: `const lastVibeCaptionRef = useRef(0)` and guard with `if (Date.now() - lastVibeCaptionRef.current < 90_000) return; lastVibeCaptionRef.current = Date.now()`. Fourth review. Escalating.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Counter Shows 1/9 Before First Click — Fifth Review
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1388: `(quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1` renders '1/9' when quoteBonus=0. The visitor has not clicked anything but the counter says they've found quote 1. Fix: render the counter only when quoteBonus > 0: `{quoteBonus > 0 && {quoteBonus}/{BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1}}`. The sparkle (now alive again since the mount-write was removed) should be the only invitation to click — the counter spoils the discovery.

ideas.title: WorkerCard Outer Div Is Focusable But Inert — Keyboard Users Land on a Dead Zone
ideas.description: App.jsx line 294: `<div ... tabIndex={0} role='article' ...>` — the outer card div is keyboard-focusable but has no onClick or onKeyDown handler. Pressing Enter or Space on a focused card does nothing. Keyboard users expect a focusable element to be interactive. The ℹ️ button is nested inside and handles the flip, but a user tabbing through cards will hit the card div first, press Enter expecting something, get silence, tab again to the button, press Enter again. Fix: add `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); setIsFlipped(f => !f) } }}` to the outer div, or remove its tabIndex and rely on the button.

ideas.title: lastHuddle Ref Needs a startedAt Field Before Elapsed Meeting Time Is Possible
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1151: `lastHuddle.current = { names: meetingWorkers.map(w => w.name), endedAt: null }`. When a huddle starts, the ref records names and clears endedAt, but never records when the huddle started. Review 75 proposed showing elapsed meeting time during active huddles — that idea requires a startedAt timestamp. Add `startedAt: Date.now()` when meetingWorkers.length >= 2. This is a prerequisite data fix; the elapsed time display is one `useState + setInterval` step after.

ideas.title: The Coffee Corner Has Secret CSS Vibes That Deserve to Be Known
ideas.description: Office.css lines 1667–1672: the coffee corner label's ::after pseudo-element changes with vibe — 'crushing' → 'TRIPLE SHOT', 'on-fire' → 'STRONG. NOW.', 'slow-day' → '...zzz', 'after-hours' → '(decaf? jk)'. The steam animation also changes speed per vibe. This is invisible to anyone reading the JSX — it lives entirely in CSS and can only be found by reading the stylesheet or noticing it in the UI. Confirmed in the live screenshot at midnight-crushing: 'COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT'. This is the kind of detail that makes a product feel alive. Document it in a code comment near the coffee-corner JSX so future developers don't accidentally remove it during a CSS refactor.

ideas.title: Vibe Weather Metaphor Ignores Time of Day — 'Clear Skies' at Midnight Is Surreal
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 112: VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS maps 'crushing' to '☀️ Clear skies — team at full strength'. At midnight during a crushing vibe this appears in the corner of a purple starfield office. The ☀️ sun emoji against a midnight-purple floor is a small but jarring collision. An optional time-of-day layer: if isAfterHours, swap the crushing weather to '🌟 Crushing it under the stars' and 'in-flow' to '🌙 Flowing through the night'. This is a 10-line addition and it makes the weather label feel as alive as the rest of the after-hours office.

ideas.title: Bloberto Should Have a Vibe × Time-of-Day Matrix for Speech Bubbles
ideas.description: The current Bloberto speech bubble system (VIBE_QUOTES) is vibe-keyed but not time-aware. At midnight the crushing vibe produces a generic 'The team is on fire' style quote. We saw in the live screenshot a perfectly contextual one: 'Wait you shipped at THIS hour?! Legends. 🏆' — this is after-hours + crushing awareness and it's the most human moment in the app. Expand this into a full 5 (vibe) × 2 (day/after-hours) matrix in VIBE_TRANSITION_QUOTES or a new BLOBERTO_SPEECH_MATRIX constant. Night-mode crushing needs at least 3 options: 'Wait you shipped at THIS hour?! Legends.', 'Who authorized overtime? (I did.)', '3am commit pushed. History will remember this.'

theme: The memo wraps nothing, the clock lies to the window, and the ficus has been grieving in silence for twelve reviews
visual_observations: App.jsx:655 — `useTimeTick(60_000)` called at App root with return value discarded. Forces a whole-app re-render every 60 seconds including WorkerCard, StatsBar, ActivityLog, and all children — with no memoization guard against this tick.

App.jsx:1257,1273-1274,1291 — `activityEntries={activityLog.filter(e => e.worker === w.name).slice(-3)}` passes a newly allocated array to WorkerCard on every render. WorkerCard is React.memo'd but memo uses shallow prop comparison — a new array reference always triggers a re-render. The memo wrapper is completely bypassed.

Office.jsx:783-789 — WindowElement useEffect: `setInterval(() => { const h = getHour(); setHour(prev => ...) }, 60000)` anchored to page-load time. WallClock got the msUntilNextMinute fix in review 76. WindowElement missed it. If the page loads at 19:59:45, the sky shows 'golden hour' until 20:00:45, then snaps to 'dusk' 45 seconds late.

App.jsx:1082 — `isAfterHours = currentHour < 5 || currentHour >= 20` — EXPANDED since last review. Hours 20-23 now get the after-hours greeting correctly. This is a genuine partial fix. However: App.jsx:1152 still reads `HONORIFICS[isAfterHours ? 'after-hours' : ...]` — the honorific is STILL overridden to 'night owl' at hours 20-23, including for 'crushing' vibe at 10pm.

Office.jsx:747 — `Math.floor(Date.now() / 12000) % length` — idle bubble emoji picker still uses wall-clock time as index. At any given 12-second window, all idle workers display the same positional index from their respective arrays simultaneously. Carlos and Maya still 'think' in unison.

Office.jsx:1188-1200 — vibeCaption useEffect fires on every vibe change with no cooldown guard. isFirstVibe skips the mount; nothing skips rapid oscillations. A team bouncing between 'in-flow' and 'slow-day' every poll cycle shows the caption overlay every 30 seconds.
App.jsx:1388 — `(quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1` renders '1/9' when quoteBonus=0. The visitor has not clicked anything but the counter says they found quote #1.

Office.jsx:1111,1114-1122 — ficus milestone useEffect: the early `return` at line 1120 exits before `prevPlantStageRef.current = plantStage` at line 1122. The ref stays at 'seedling' after initial mount. When the streak drops and plantStage reverts to 'seedling', `'seedling' !== 'seedling'` is false — grief toast cannot fire. Twelve reviews. Structural.

App.jsx:294 — `<div ... tabIndex={0} role='article' ...>` outer WorkerCard div is keyboard-focusable but has no onKeyDown or onClick. Pressing Enter or Space does nothing. Keyboard users land on a dead zone.

Office.jsx:1278 — `avg task progress` renders unconditionally when any worker is active. With exactly one worker, the whiteboard calls their number an 'average'. First flagged in review 60. This is review 77. That is seventeen consecutive reviews for a one-word ternary.

fixed_since_last_review.title: isAfterHours Extended to Cover Hours 20-23 for Greeting
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx:1082 — `isAfterHours = currentHour < 5 || currentHour >= 20`. The greeting now correctly shows '🌃 Burning the midnight oil' or '🦉 Still at it' for evening hours instead of 'Good evening'. Partial fix — the honorific lookup still overrides the vibe-based honorific at these hours.

ideas.title: WorkerCard React.memo Is Completely Bypassed by Inline filter().slice() Props
ideas.description: App.jsx lines 1257, 1273, 1291 — `activityEntries={activityLog.filter(e => e.worker === w.name).slice(-3)}` is called inside the workers-grid .map() on every render. This allocates a fresh array reference on each call regardless of whether the underlying entries changed. WorkerCard is wrapped with React.memo (line 271) which uses shallow comparison — a new array reference always triggers a re-render even with identical contents. The memo optimization is structurally defeated. Every 30-second poll cycle and every 60-second tick causes all 5 WorkerCards to re-render from scratch. Fix: compute entries once outside the map loop as a Map keyed by worker name — `const entriesByWorker = useMemo(() => { const m = {}; for (const e of activityLog) { if (e.worker) (m[e.worker] = m[e.worker] || []).push(e) } return m }, [activityLog])` — then pass `(entriesByWorker[w.name] || []).slice(-3)`. Alternatively: use a stable slice by wrapping in useMemo per worker. WHY: React.memo is a promise that a component won't re-render unless its data changes. Passing inline filter().slice() makes that promise impossible to keep. This affects every single card on every poll cycle.

ideas.title: WindowElement Interval Not Clock-Aligned — WallClock's Fixed Twin Is Still Running Late
ideas.description: Office.jsx:783-789 — `setInterval(() => { const h = getHour(); setHour(prev => prev === h ? prev : h) }, 60000)` is anchored to page-load time, not to the real minute boundary. WallClock got the exact fix it needed in review 76: `const msUntilNextMinute = 60000 - (Date.now() % 60000); const timeout = setTimeout(() => { setTime(new Date()); id = setInterval(..., 60000) }, msUntilNextMinute)`. The same pattern applies to WindowElement's useEffect — just swap `setTime` for `setHour(getHour())`. If the page loads at 19:59:45, the sky shows golden-hour orange until 20:00:45, then snaps to the night-purple gradient 45 seconds late. This is the WallClock twin bug identified in review 76 and still not merged. The pattern is already in the codebase — copy-paste fix. WHY: the sky gradient and the wall clock tell complementary stories about what time it is. Having one accurate and one drifting breaks the clock metaphor at exactly the moment a visitor is most likely to notice it.

ideas.title: Honorific Still Overridden to 'night owl' at Hours 20-23 Despite Crushing Vibe
ideas.description: App.jsx:1082 — isAfterHours was correctly extended to `currentHour < 5 || currentHour >= 20` (partial fix confirmed). The greeting now fires correctly. But App.jsx:1152 — `HONORIFICS[isAfterHours ? 'after-hours' : (teamVibe.key === 'after-hours' ? 'slow-day' : teamVibe.key)] ?? 'boss'` — still uses isAfterHours to force the 'after-hours' key at hours 20-23, returning 'night owl 🌙' regardless of team vibe. At 10pm during a crushing sprint the header reads 'Burning the midnight oil, night owl 🌙 · Crushing It'. The honorific and the vibe badge contradict each other. Fix: remove the isAfterHours override from the honorific lookup entirely — `HONORIFICS[teamVibe.key] ?? 'boss'`. The greeting already handles the time context with '🌃 Burning the midnight oil'. The honorific should reinforce the energy, not override it. WHY: 'Burning the midnight oil, LEGEND 🔥' is a coherent and energizing thought. 'Burning the midnight oil, night owl 🌙' during peak productivity is a small but distinct deflation.

ideas.title: idleBubble Global Clock Makes All Workers Daydream in Unison — Fifth Review
ideas.description: Office.jsx:747 — `(WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES[worker.id] || DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES)[Math.floor(Date.now() / 12000) % length]` — the index is derived from wall-clock Unix time divided by 12 seconds. Every idle worker computes the same index at the same instant. At 12:00:00 Carlos shows ☕ (index 0), Maya shows 💜 (index 0), Sofia shows 🔍 (index 0). At 12:00:12 they all advance together. WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES was correctly added in review 71 to give each character their own emoji array. The synchronized picker throws that work away. One-line fix: seed the index with a character-specific offset — `Math.floor((Date.now() + (worker.id?.charCodeAt(0) ?? 0) * 4000) / 12000) % length`. This drifts each character's 12-second window by their first-character code multiplied by 4 seconds, making their thoughts genuinely independent. WHY: idle bubbles are thought windows. Synchronized thought windows look like a system glitch. Staggered windows look like five different minds.

ideas.title: vibeCaption Has No Cooldown — Oscillating Teams See the Caption Every 30 Seconds — Fifth Review
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1188-1200 — `setVibeCaption(CAPTIONS[vibe] || null)` fires on every [vibe] change. The isFirstVibe.current guard only prevents the initial mount. A team sitting on the boundary between 'in-flow' (40-70% working) and 'slow-day' (below 40%) — one worker toggling every poll — generates a caption every 30 seconds: '⚡ In the zone' → '☁️ Easy does it' → '⚡ In the zone'. Add `const lastVibeCaptionRef = useRef(0)` and guard: `if (Date.now() - lastVibeCaptionRef.current < 90_000) return; lastVibeCaptionRef.current = Date.now()` before the setVibeCaption call. 90-second minimum interval between captions. WHY: the vibe caption is a punctuation mark, not a ticker. One word that announces a shift. That word loses all meaning when it appears every 30 seconds.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Counter Shows 1/9 on Fresh Load Before Any Click — Sixth Review
ideas.description: App.jsx:1388 — `{(quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1}/{BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1}` — BONUS_TAGLINES has 8 entries so denominator is 9. On fresh load quoteBonus=0, so `(0 % 9) + 1 = 1`. The visitor has not clicked anything but the UI announces they have found quote 1 of 9. The denominator bundles the default vibe tagline into the discovery set which is not a bonus. Fix: render the counter only after the first click — `{quoteBonus > 0 && {quoteBonus}/{BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1}}`. The footer sparkle (now correctly preserved until first click since review 76) should be the only invitation — the counter should reward the action, not precede it. WHY: discovery counters are motivating only when accurate. A counter that starts at 1/9 before the visitor has done anything teaches them that UI numbers here are decorative.

ideas.title: WorkerCard Outer Div Is Keyboard-Focusable but Dead — Enter and Space Do Nothing
ideas.description: App.jsx:294 — `<div ref={cardRef} className={classes.join(' ')} tabIndex={0} role='article' ...>`. The outer div is focusable (tabIndex=0) and has a semantic role, but has no onKeyDown handler. When a keyboard user tabs to a worker card and presses Enter or Space — the natural expectation for an interactive focusable element — nothing happens. The flip is triggered only by the nested ℹ️ button, which requires a second tab stop. Fix: add `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); setIsFlipped(f => !f) } }}` to the outer div. This makes the card flip on keyboard activation at the outer div level, consistent with what a sighted user can do by clicking anywhere on the card. WHY: keyboard users and screen reader users are real Bloberto's Office visitors. A focusable element that doesn't respond to keyboard activation is an accessibility contract broken in plain sight.

ideas.title: Whiteboard Says 'avg task progress' When One Person Is the Entire Team — Review 60 Through Review 77
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1278 — `avg task progress` renders unconditionally whenever workingWorkers.length > 0. When exactly one worker is active, their progress is the average by definition — but the label still says 'avg'. This was first flagged in review 60 as a 'should' item with the note 'The Whiteboard Calls It an Average With One Person in the Room'. That was seventeen review cycles ago. The fix is a single ternary: `{workingWorkers.length === 1 ? 'task progress' : 'avg task progress'}`. One JSX change. One word difference. Zero logic change. WHY: this has survived seventeen consecutive reviews, which means it keeps getting triaged out in favor of larger items. But it is visible every time a solo worker is active — which is a common state — and it says something factually wrong on a UI element that is supposed to be the most prominent data surface in the office. If this doesn't land in review 77, it should be assigned with a deadline.

ideas.title: Ficus Grief Toast Architecturally Blocked — prevPlantStageRef Never Updated on Growth — Twelve Reviews
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1114-1122 — the ficus milestone useEffect: `if (prevPlantStageRef.current !== plantStage && plantStage !== 'seedling') { setPlantMilestone(true); ...; return () => {...} }`. The early `return` exits the effect body before reaching `prevPlantStageRef.current = plantStage` at line 1122. The ref stays at 'seedling' after initial mount. When the streak drops and plantStage reverts to 'seedling', the check evaluates `'seedling' !== 'seedling'` = false and no grief toast fires. The fix requires two lines added inside the if-branch: (1) `prevPlantStageRef.current = plantStage` before the early return so growth events update the ref; (2) a separate `else if (prevPlantStageRef.current !== 'seedling' && plantStage === 'seedling')` branch to set plantToast with a grief message. Flagged since review 65. Twelve consecutive reviews. The structural cause has been clearly identified since review 74. WHY: the ficus growing has fanfare and a toast. The ficus dying has silence. The asymmetry makes the streak system feel like a one-way ratchet rather than a living thing. The grief is supposed to happen — the code just structurally prevents it from firing.

ideas.title: ErrorBoundary Swallows All Errors Silently — No Logging, No Recovery, No componentDidCatch
ideas.description: App.jsx:7-20 — the ErrorBoundary class has `getDerivedStateFromError()` but no `componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo)`. This means any render error in the component tree is caught, the UI switches to 'Something went wrong — refresh the page', and the actual error is permanently lost. No stack trace. No component name. No way to reproduce. The error message itself offers no retry button — the only recovery is a full page refresh. Fix in two steps: (1) add `componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo) { console.error('[Bloberto ErrorBoundary]', error, errorInfo) }` — at minimum the stack trace appears in the developer console; (2) add a retry button to the fallback render: `<button onClick={() => this.setState({ hasError: false })} ...>↺ Try again</button>`. A third improvement would show the error.message in non-production environments. WHY: an error boundary that catches without logging is production-blind. When the office breaks for a real user, the developer gets a bug report that says 'Something went wrong' with nothing else. The boundary should be the last line of defense and the first source of truth.

ideas.title: WindowElement Interval Wakes Up Drunk — WallClock's Twin Bug Still Unpatched
ideas.description: Office.jsx:783 — `setInterval(() => { const h = getHour(); setHour(prev => ...) }, 60000)` is anchored to page-load time with no boundary alignment. WallClock at Office.jsx:1004-1009 already has the correct pattern: `const msUntilNextMinute = 60000 - (Date.now() % 60000); const timeout = setTimeout(() => { setTime(new Date()); id = setInterval(..., 60000) }, msUntilNextMinute)`. Apply the identical three-line pattern to WindowElement's useEffect: compute `msUntilNextMinute`, use `setTimeout` to land on the real boundary, then start the interval. Cleanup must call `clearTimeout(timeout)` and `clearInterval(id)`. If the page loads at 19:59:45, the sky shows golden-hour orange for 45 extra seconds before snapping to evening purple — while the WallClock correctly shows 20:00. Two clocks in the same room telling different stories. The fix is a copy-paste from a neighbor function. WHY: flagged in reviews #76 and #77; the WallClock was patched immediately, its twin was not. Every hour boundary between those two clocks produces a visible desync.

ideas.title: Honorific Lock Forces 'night owl' During Crushing Sprints at 10pm
ideas.description: App.jsx:1152 — `HONORIFICS[isAfterHours ? 'after-hours' : (teamVibe.key === 'after-hours' ? 'slow-day' : teamVibe.key)]`. The `isAfterHours` guard at line 1082 was correctly extended to `currentHour < 5 || currentHour >= 20` — the GREETING now fires correctly ('Burning the midnight oil' at 10pm). But this same isAfterHours flag is immediately used to override the HONORIFIC, mapping any hour ≥ 20 to 'night owl 🌙' regardless of team vibe. A crushing sprint at 10pm reads 'Burning the midnight oil, night owl 🌙 · Crushing It'. The greeting and vibe badge agree on the energy. The honorific contradicts both. Fix: remove the isAfterHours override from the honorific lookup entirely — `HONORIFICS[teamVibe.key === 'after-hours' ? 'slow-day' : teamVibe.key] ?? 'boss'`. The greeting already carries time context; the honorific should reinforce the vibe. 'Burning the midnight oil, LEGEND 🔥' is coherent and energizing. 'Burning the midnight oil, night owl 🌙' deflates the exact moment it should amplify. WHY: confirmed visually in review #76 screenshot. Flagged in reviews #76 and #77. The partial fix made the greeting right and left the honorific wrong.

ideas.title: React.memo on WorkerCard Is Bypassed on Every Poll — Inline filter().slice() Breaks the Contract
ideas.description: App.jsx:1280 — `activityEntries={activityLog.filter(e => e.worker === w.name).slice(-3)}` is called inside the workers-grid `.map()` callback on every render. Every 30-second poll and every 60-second `useTimeTick()` tick re-runs this loop, allocating fresh array references per worker. WorkerCard is wrapped with `React.memo` (line 271) using shallow prop comparison — a new array reference always triggers a re-render even with identical contents. With 5 workers and a 30s poll interval, this produces at minimum 10 unnecessary WorkerCard re-renders per minute. Fix: compute the entries map ONCE outside the `.map()` loop using `useMemo` keyed to `activityLog`: `const entriesByWorker = useMemo(() => { const m = {}; for (const e of activityLog) { if (e.worker) (m[e.worker] ??= []).push(e) } return m }, [activityLog])` — then pass `(entriesByWorker[w.name] ?? []).slice(-3)`. This stable reference lets `React.memo` do its job. WHY: flagged in review #77. React.memo is a performance contract. Inline array allocations make that contract structurally impossible to honor.

ideas.title: idleBubble Emoji Picker Is Still a Global Metronome — Five Reviews
ideas.description: Office.jsx:747 — `Math.floor(Date.now() / 12000) % length` — the index is derived from Unix time divided by 12 seconds, shared by every Character instance. At any given 12-second window, all idle workers display the same positional index from their respective emoji arrays simultaneously. Carlos, Maya, and Sofia 'think' in perfect 12-second lockstep. WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES (added in review #71) gives each worker a unique array of emojis — the intent was character-specific thought moments. The synchronized picker discards that intent entirely. One-line fix: seed the index with a per-worker offset derived from their id — `Math.floor((Date.now() + (worker.id?.charCodeAt(0) ?? 0) * 4000) / 12000) % length`. This drifts each character's 12-second window by their first character code × 4 seconds, making idle thoughts genuinely independent. WHY: fifth consecutive review flagging this. The fix has been specified for three reviews. The work to make WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES character-specific was done in review #71; this one-liner is the payoff.

ideas.title: vibeCaption Has No Cooldown — Five Reviews of the Same Overlay Spam
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1193-1204 — the vibeCaption useEffect fires on every `[vibe]` dependency change with only an `isFirstVibe` mount guard. No minimum interval between display events. A team hovering between 'in-flow' (40–70% working) and 'slow-day' (below 40%) — one worker toggling every 30-second poll — generates the '⚡ In the zone' → '☁️ Easy does it' overlay loop indefinitely. The caption has been reduced to ambient noise. Fix: `const lastVibeCaptionRef = useRef(0)` and at the top of the effect: `if (Date.now() - lastVibeCaptionRef.current < 90_000) return; lastVibeCaptionRef.current = Date.now()`. 90 seconds minimum between captions. WHY: fifth consecutive review. The caption is a punctuation mark, not a ticker. It should announce a shift, not annotate noise. The proposed fix has been in the review file since review #74.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Counter Has Two Bugs: Starts at 1/9 AND Wraps Back to 1/9 After Discovery
ideas.description: App.jsx:1388 — `{(quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1}/{BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1}` — Bug #1 (six reviews): on fresh load quoteBonus=0, formula gives `(0 % 9) + 1 = 1`, shows '1/9'. Visitor hasn't clicked anything. Bug #2 (NEW, first time flagged): when quoteBonus reaches 9 (one full rotation through all 8 bonus quotes plus the cycling reset), the formula gives `(9 % 9) + 1 = 1`, looping back to '1/9'. A user who has discovered all 8 bonus quotes and clicks once more is told they found quote #1. The discovery arc resets silently with no ceremony or acknowledgment. Fix for both: gate the counter on `quoteBonus > 0` and show the raw bonus count capped at `BONUS_TAGLINES.length` — `{quoteBonus > 0 && {Math.min(quoteBonus, BONUS_TAGLINES.length)}/{BONUS_TAGLINES.length}}`. WHY: discovery counters are contracts about progress. Starting wrong trains distrust; looping without notice teaches that completion is meaningless.

ideas.title: Whiteboard Calls One Person's Progress an Average — Eighteen Reviews
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1291 — `avg task progress` renders unconditionally when any worker is active. When one worker is active, their progress IS their progress. Calling it an average of one data point is mathematically correct and socially wrong — Sofia carrying the whole team deserves 'task progress', not 'avg task progress'. Fix: `{workingWorkers.length === 1 ? 'task progress' : 'avg task progress'}`. One ternary. One word difference. Zero logic change. First flagged in review #60. This is review #78. Eighteen consecutive review cycles. The fix is a single JSX operator and has been written out verbatim in at least four prior reviews. WHY: this is the oldest unfixed idea in the file. Its persistence across eighteen reviews means either the triage process cannot handle one-word changes, or this file is not being read before each development cycle. Either is worth naming.

ideas.title: ProgressBar Dead updatedAt Prop — Four Reviews, Broken Contract in the Signature
ideas.description: App.jsx:195 — `function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt })` — `updatedAt` is destructured and passed at line 350 (`updatedAt={worker.updated_at}`). Inside the function body, the identifier `updatedAt` does not appear once. `startedAt` correctly drives ETA calculation. `updatedAt` was presumably intended for a staleness signal but was never wired. Flagged in reviews #71, #72, #74, #77. Two paths: (A) remove `updatedAt` from the destructure and the call site — clean contract; (B) use it — render a subtle 'stalled' indicator when `updatedAt` is set, `progress < 100`, and `Date.now() - new Date(updatedAt).getTime() > 1_800_000` (30 minutes of no change). Path B converts dead code into the staleness warning the parameter was always meant to carry. WHY: a function parameter that accepts data and ignores it is a broken contract. Future developers who see `updatedAt` in props will assume it does something. It does not.

ideas.title: Ficus Grief Toast Structurally Impossible — prevPlantStageRef Never Updated on Growth — Thirteen Reviews
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1114-1122 — the ficus useEffect: `if (prevPlantStageRef.current !== plantStage && plantStage !== 'seedling') { ...; return () => { clearTimeout(t1); clearTimeout(t2) } }; prevPlantStageRef.current = plantStage`. The `return () => {...}` inside the if-branch is the cleanup function — returning it exits the useEffect callback. The assignment `prevPlantStageRef.current = plantStage` at line 1122 is AFTER the return and never executes on growth events. The ref stays at 'seedling' after the initial mount. When vibeStreak drops and plantStage reverts to 'seedling': `'seedling' !== 'seedling'` = false. No grief fires. Fix: add `prevPlantStageRef.current = plantStage` INSIDE the if-branch before the return, and add a grief branch: `else if (prevPlantStageRef.current !== 'seedling' && plantStage === 'seedling') { setPlantToast(true); const t = setTimeout(() => setPlantToast(false), 3000); return () => clearTimeout(t) }`. Two insertions that fix a thirteen-review wound. WHY: the ficus growing has fanfare and a toast. The ficus dying has nothing. The asymmetry makes the streak feel like a decoration rather than a living thing that can be lost.

ideas.title: TASK_TAG_DEFS Is Missing a Refactor/Performance Tag — Engineering's Hidden Work
ideas.description: App.jsx:164-172 — TASK_TAG_DEFS covers Bug, Ship, Design, QA, Data, Docs, Plan. Tasks like 'refactor auth module', 'optimize cold start', 'improve query performance', 'benchmark load time', 'clean up legacy code' match ZERO patterns and produce no tags. Refactoring work is a substantial portion of engineering time — often Carlos's and Dave's highest-impact sessions — and it is entirely invisible to the tag filter system. Add: `{ pattern: /refactor|optimiz|benchmark|perf|cleanup|clean.?up|debt|legacy/i, label: 'Refactor', emoji: '🔵', className: 'task-tag--refactor' }` after the Docs entry. Add `.task-tag--refactor { background: rgba(59,130,246,0.12); color: #3b82f6; }` in App.css. WHY: the tag system exists to give the dashboard instant semantic context. An entire category of engineering work being invisible tells the tag system was designed for feature work only. Refactor work is the engineering iceberg — the dashboard should at least acknowledge the waterline.

The idle bubble timer is per-worker now but the emoji content index is still Math.floor((Date.now() + charCode * 4000) / 12000) — a global 12-second world clock. Workers just have different offsets, not independent clocks.

Footer counter no longer shows 1/9 on fresh load. Fixed. But after all 8 bonus quotes wrap, quoteBonus=9 produces counter 1/9 while showing bonus[0] which is quote 2 of 9 total. Counter and content out of sync.
ProgressBar receives updatedAt in its function signature but never uses it. The prop is destructured into the void.

fixed_since_last_review.title: WallClock Now Clock-Aligned with msUntilNextMinute Pattern

ideas.title: Window Sky Hours 20-23: dusk Label on a Night Gradient With No Stars
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 811: const timeLabel uses a final else that labels hours 20-23 as dusk. But getGradient(20) returns linear-gradient(to bottom, #4c1d95, #312e81) — deep purple night. And isMidnight is hour >= 0 && hour <= 4 only, so hours 20-23 get zero stars. The window says dusk while looking like the void of space. Fix: rename isMidnight to isDeepNight and extend it to hour >= 20 || hour <= 4. Rename the timeLabel for hours 20-23 from dusk to night. Two small changes. This has appeared in every review since 75. Six reviews. It must end here.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Counter Wraps to 1/9 But Shows Bonus[0] — Content and Counter Out of Sync
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1396: counter is (quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1. With 8 bonus taglines denominator is 9. When quoteBonus reaches 9 (after clicking through all bonuses): counter shows (9 % 9) + 1 = 1, so 1/9. But content is BONUS_TAGLINES[(9-1) % 8] = BONUS_TAGLINES[0] — the first bonus which is quote 2 of 9 total. Counter says 1, content is 2. Out of sync. Fix: derive the counter from the content position. The shown quote index is (quoteBonus - 1) % 8 for bonus quotes. Its position in the full 9-quote sequence is ((quoteBonus - 1) % 8) + 2 (since vibe tagline is 1). Use that as numerator.

ideas.title: idleBubble Emoji Content Index Still Rides the Global Wall Clock
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 747: content index is Math.floor((Date.now() + (worker.id?.charCodeAt(0) ?? 0) * 4000) / 12000) % length. The bubble TIMER is now per-worker via wanderIdx stagger, which is good. But the content selection is still a global 12-second wall clock with per-worker offsets. At any given second you can predict every worker emoji from the formula. Fix: inside the idle micro-bubble useEffect, store a random content index in a useRef and advance it randomly when the bubble shows, completely decoupling content from wall time.

ideas.title: ProgressBar updatedAt Prop Is Declared But Never Referenced Inside the Component
ideas.description: App.jsx around line 198: function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt }). The updatedAt parameter is destructured but never used in the component body. The ETA calculation uses startedAt only. The formatDuration call that uses updatedAt happens in WorkerCard, not ProgressBar. The prop exists in the contract but is honored by no one. Either remove it from the signature, or use it as a fallback when startedAt is absent: const workingMs = (startedAt || updatedAt) ? Date.now() - new Date(startedAt || updatedAt).getTime() : null. This has been open since review 75.

ideas.title: wanderIdx Caps at 4 — With Five Idle Workers Two Share the Same Bubble Interval
ideas.description: Office.jsx idle workers render: const wIdx = (i % 4) + 1. Worker index 0 and worker index 4 both get wIdx = 1. Their idle bubble timer is 8000 + 1 * 3000 = 11000ms. Both fire at the same moment. With all five workers idle (Carlos Maya Dave Sofia Luna), two will always synchronize their bubble display defeating the stagger intent. Fix: use a hash of the worker ID for a stable but diverse offset instead of wanderIdx, or simply let i run freely without the modulo cap since there are only ever 5 workers.

ideas.title: WorkerCard Outer Div Has tabIndex={0} With No Interaction Handlers
ideas.description: App.jsx line 302: the outer div has tabIndex={0} and role=article making it a keyboard tab stop. But there is no onKeyDown, no onClick, nothing. Keyboard users tab to the card and pressing Enter or Space does nothing. The inner info button handles the flip correctly, but the card itself is a dead focus stop. Fix: add onKeyDown to the outer div that calls setIsFlipped(true) on Enter or Space, matching what the info button does. Or remove tabIndex={0} and expose the card exclusively through its button children. The current state is a silent accessibility failure.

ideas.title: TASK_TAG_DEFS Missing Refactor Category — Engineering Invisible Work
ideas.description: App.jsx lines 165-177: TASK_TAG_DEFS covers Bug Ship Design QA Data Docs Plan. No pattern for refactor or performance work. Tasks like refactor auth layer, performance optimization, clean up tech debt produce no tag. Engineering's most honest work is invisible to the dashboard. Fix: add pattern /refactor|cleanup|clean.?up|tech.?debt|optimize|performance|perf/i with label Refactor, emoji wrench, className task-tag--refactor, and a distinct CSS color like #14b8a6 teal.

ideas.title: Character Non-Manager pingReaction Sets bubble State That Is Never Displayed
ideas.description: Office.jsx lines 533-537: the pingReaction useEffect runs for ALL Character variants and calls setBubble({ quote: pingReaction, show: true }). But for non-manager workers the speech bubble renders from the pingReaction prop directly at line 638, not from bubble.quote. The setBubble call for non-managers triggers a state update and re-render whose result is never read. The setTimeout to reset bubble.show also fires 3 seconds later for another orphan re-render. Fix: guard the setBubble and timerRef block with an early return if variant !== manager.

ideas.title: lastHuddle Ref Missing startedAt — Elapsed Meeting Time Is Architecturally Blocked
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1172: const lastHuddle = useRef({ names: [], endedAt: null }). When a huddle starts at line 1173: lastHuddle.current = { names: meetingWorkers.map(w => w.name), endedAt: null }. No startedAt is ever set. ConferenceTable cannot show elapsed time during an active huddle because start time was never captured. Fix: add startedAt: Date.now() when setting the active huddle. ConferenceTable can then compute Date.now() - lastHuddle.current.startedAt to render a live meeting timer like In huddle for 8m. Flagged in review 76.

ideas.title: Ghost OOO Bubble Knows the Role But Not the Last Deed
ideas.description: Office.jsx ghost variant around line 702: the OOO bubble shows role and last-seen time. But activityEntries is already passed in and lastEvent is already fetched for relTime. The message field is right there. If the last activity was Shipped auth migration the ghost bubble could whisper: Last shipped: Shipped auth migration. Each ghost gets a eulogy instead of just a timestamp. One extra line: render lastEvent?.message to give absent workers a story rather than a vanishing act.

ideas.title: Whiteboard Doodles Are Static — They Should Escalate With vibeStreak
ideas.description: Office.jsx around line 1313: five static ASCII faces, one per vibe, never change. But Office already receives vibeStreak as a prop. At streak 1 the crushing face is calm. At streak 5 it gets excited. At streak 10 legendary tier it should be full chaos energy. At streak 1 on-fire is tense. At streak 5 it escalates. Let the whiteboard doodle breathe with the room. It is the emotional thermometer of the office floor and right now it has three settings: fine, fine, and fine.
